The reality of the world we live in today is that firearms can be manufactured from raw materials using widely available tools.

There are CAD files easily available for complete firearms and magazines which are freely accessible on the internet. 3D printed firearms such as the FGC nine have already been used by non-state groups internationally. And there is instructional material for improvised firearms, which has existed since at least the 1990s.

Legislation that focuses on restricting compliant owners cannot stop individuals who choose to ignore the law from manufacturing or acquiring firearms outside of the legal system. Criminals do not register firearms. They do not serialize parts, and they do not comply with licensing requirements. Number two, the illegal importation is the primary risk vector.

Further restrictions on licensed owners do nothing to address this vector. Two, the laws are being broken by those without licenses.

It is therefore unreasonable to conclude that further restrictions on compliance citizens will reduce firearms harm any further.

If the objective is public safety legislation must demonstrably target those who pose risk, not who those who already complied my recommendations, the administration of the Firearms Act. There is widespread concern within the firearms community about the suitability of the New Zealand police as the administering authority for firearms licensing, evidence and prosecution of individual New Zealand police officer criminality and corruption alongside reports of inconsistent decision making.

Disproportionate enforcement imp punitive responses to minor or unrelated incidents have eroded trust and confidence in the system. And the New Zealand police. Our recommendation would be to establish an independent firearms authority separate from the police responsible for licensing, renewals, compliance audits, and suspension and revocation processes.

The authority should include substantial representation from experienced licensed firearms owners who have proven themselves to be the safest group in New Zealand with firearms.
